title: Use Formula Column as Date
excerpt: It would be great if you could use a formula column to auto-populate dates and then be able to create automations for notifications based on the date in that formula column. For example, we would like to enter the final …

posts.author: dspry (David Spry)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be great if you could use a formula column to auto-populate dates and then be able to create automations for notifications based on the date in that formula column. For example, we would like to enter the final due date for a project and use a formula column to auto populate another date that can be actioned against for notifications or other automations.

posts.author: ampwst (Wesley Teerlink)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes!! So glad I found this thread. I have been trying for several days to find a work around with no luck yet.
I am trying to see some items in Calendar view but Calendar will only show items with a true “Date” column type even though the output of my formula is a date it cannot be seen there. It is really inhibiting my ability to create automations in Monday.com overall.
I am having the same problem with formulas with number outputs. I would love to have a function that forces the formula output to a specific data/column type.
Without a way to use formula columns with automations and calendar etc… they are not very useful. Can we know what the plan is here? Can we hope for some sort of solution going forward?

posts.author: Joon (Ng Joon Leong)
posts.cookedHtml: I totally agree with you! It would be really nice to have that feature where the output of the formula can be used as an argument in an automation/integration.

posts.author: ebannister (Elliot Bannister)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my vote to this. We use a formula column to generate dates from text in another column. It would be awesome to be able to use these formula-dates in views like the calendar.

posts.author: katie_e (Katie Edwards)
posts.cookedHtml: I think this would be a really useful feature. The ‘When Date Arrives’ automation exclusively works with date columns, but there are times when I would really like to send out automated reminders to team members when a date that has been created through a formula arrives instead. I’ve had a nosy and not found a work around for this yet, so I’m hoping this is a feature that Monday will consider. Thank you!

posts.author: Shadster86 (Shadie)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any update on this feature? It’s difficult to make a work back schedule as subitems without being able to recognize the formula date as an actual Date column!

posts.author: erikm (Erik Peterson)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to vote for this feature also. Without it, the automations are very limited when involving dates, or result in manual input being required when it should not be needed. I was able to do this with Smartsheet without issue, and this has been the most disappointing thing with Monday. I have seen several forums requesting this over the past few years now. Why the delay for what would be such a useful feature?

posts.author: dani-relational (Dani L)
posts.cookedHtml: +1. I’m really surprised this is not available yet.

posts.author: Leigh_73 (Leigh Skilton)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature. Seems a valuable addition for lots of users.

posts.author: ieller (Ian Eller)
posts.cookedHtml: Not only would it be good for automations but in graphical dashboards as well. Currently, a formula column returning a formatted date doesn’t allow you to group by month or sort the dates properly when adjusting settings on a graph. You ether get ALL the dates, or if you change your formula output to MMM YYYY you get values grouped by month but then if you sort X ascending its alphabetical, not chronological.

posts.author: popitupapi (Popi)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this as well. For everything that can be done in the app, this seems fairly reasonable.

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey,

Since the formula column result can’t currently be interpreted as a date, one potential solution is to copy the result into a date column.

This can be achieved using our Column to Column app. The app supports a variety of date formats (including those with time zones) and also if you reference mirror date columns in a formula.

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dspry,

Regarding your question about using formula column as a date and creating automations from it.

If you are looking for a well rounded approach to addressing this, we’ve just published a detailed walkthrough in the community that directly addresses this challenge. It shows you how to use your formula column results to kick off automations.

You might find it really helpful: Copy formula result to other columns and trigger automations

Hope this helps you out!

posts.author: michael_mbg (Michael)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would so helpful!

Description:
Enable Formula Columns that calculate dates to be recognized and treated as standard Date Columns. This would allow these formula-generated dates to fully integrate with all date-specific features and automations.

Why This Is Important:
Currently, Formula Columns can calculate dates, but these dates cannot be used in:

  • Automations (e.g., reminders, status changes based on date)
  • Calendar views
  • Timeline or Gantt views
  • Dependencies
  • Dashboards and widgets that require Date Columns

Proposed Solution:
Allow Formula Columns that return a date value to be treated as Date Columns for all features and automations.

Impact:
This would enable workflows where one “master date” can drive multiple dependent dates via formulas, without losing functionality. It simplifies project management and reduces manual updates.
